---
title: Foundations of Software Development in Health and Social Care
description: |
  **Delivery mode:** Online (distance learning) 
  
  **Start date:** Years 1, 2, and 3 | Term 2 - Block 4 
  
  **Credits:** 10 
site-url: https://www.drps.ed.ac.uk/current/dpt/cxhein11066.htm
image: uoe.jpg
#image-alt: |
categories: [code, data, context, postgraduate]
open-graph: true
twitter-card: true
# will inherit title, description, image
# to change ANYTHING about card, need to fill in EVERYTHING below
#  title: |
#  description: |
#  image: |
#  image-alt: |
#  defaults to 500 x 500 summary: uncomment lines below for large card
#  image-width: 600 
#  image-height: 314
#  card-style: summary_large_image 
#  image-width: 500
#  image-height: 500
#  card-style: summary
draft: false
---
::: {.profile-image-float-left}
![](.jpg){fig-align="centre"}
:::

## About this course

his is an introductory course in Python programming - it should be taken by students who are absolute beginners to Python and programming practices, and it will provide a solid foundation to using programming to solve issues in health and social care.
Learn the philosophy and craft of creating sustainable, well-writtten code. Lots of practice in Python. Witness stories from code creators in H&SC. From complete basics all the way to analysing and visualising simple data.
 
## Assessment
 
Coding assessment with mini-report. 3 stars and 1 wish self-reflections. 

## Programming

Python
